name: Jellied Biscuits
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine flour, baking powder, sugar, salt, and cream of tartar.
2. Cut in shortening until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
3. Add milk; stir quickly with a fork just until mixed.
4. Drop by rounded tablespoonfuls onto a greased baking sheet.
5. Make a deep thumbprint in tops; fill each with 1 teaspoon of jelly.
6. Bake at 450 degrees for 10-12 minutes or until biscuits are browned.
"Jellied Biscuits are a pleasure to serve because they look so lovely with the jelly on them." - Marsha Ransom, Taste of Home Magazine, October/November 1994.
